attack_surface.exposure: IStrictKindEnforcer / IKindValidatable; three gates in variable.set (build, runtime probe, byte-materialization imprint); image is the sole implementor today
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Build-time literals (no trust), runtime %var% (caller-supplied), wire-arrived Data (signing-gated)
mitigations: Strict flag covered by Ed25519 canonical hash; probe gate forces materialisation which closes the lazy-passthrough bypass; image ctors are side-effect-free
gaps: _requiredKind imprint is process-local (Strict from wire only applies when receiver opts in via `as ... strict`) — threat-model accepted

attack_surface.exposure: variable.set walks public ctors of IKindValidatable types and Invokes with user-data; image is the only implementor today
attack_surface.trust_boundary: targetType is constrained to PLang type registry + IKindValidatable; user data flows as ctor arg 0
mitigations: IKindValidatable gate; type registry not attacker-controlled; image ctors are field assignment only
gaps: Forward-looking: new IKindValidatable types (audio/video planned) MUST have side-effect-free public ctors; discipline lives in convention only

attack_surface.exposure: JsonSerializer.Serialize with no options when binding a structured value `as text`
attack_surface.trust_boundary: User-opt-in via `as text`; the source structure may reach a path.@this or [Sensitive] property
mitigations: User must explicitly bind as text; resulting text usually flows to user-controlled channels
gaps: No PathJsonConverter / SensitivePropertyFilter / IgnoreCycles — pattern matches the documented test/report.cs incident; flagged by semgrep

attack_surface.exposure: Hash now returns hash.@this carrying algorithm; signing reads algorithm off value; HashDataConverter deserialises algorithm from wire
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Outer Ed25519 covers the canonical signing bytes incl. algorithm string
mitigations: Algorithm whitelist (keccak256 only) in Hash switch; algorithm string included in Ed25519 canonical hash; unknown algo returns UnsupportedAlgorithm
gaps: DigestEquals uses Span.SequenceEqual (not constant-time) — info only; pre-image resistance dwarfs timing leaks for data hash; would matter only if MAC verification is added

attack_surface.exposure: image.ValidateKind calls DetectFormat on caller-supplied bytes
attack_surface.trust_boundary: Pre-verify reachable via `set %x% = %wireBytes% as image/X strict`
mitigations: User explicit opt-in via `as image/X strict`; DetectFormat is header-only (lightweight); catch-all guard except OOM/SOE
gaps: Same opt-in surface that Width/Height already exposed; no new exposure

findings.vector: set %s% = %dictWithPath% as text — text.Convert JSON-serialises with no options
findings.preconditions: User opts into `as text`; source dict graph reaches a path.@this or a [Sensitive] property
findings.impact: path.@this graphs leak absolute filesystem paths in the rendered text; [Sensitive] fields render plaintext; cycles throw
findings.exploit_sketch: Build a dict containing a path value (e.g. result of a file.read result), `set %t% = %d% as text`, then echo %t% — the absolute path is in the output
findings.proposed_fix: Thread the context onto the call: JsonSerializer.Serialize(value, Context.App.Format.Options) (or Conversion.ContextualReadOptions(context))

findings.vector: Strict imprint is process-local — wire Strict=true does not auto-impose RequireStrictKind on receiver
findings.impact: BytesAsync runs without strict check on receiver even if sender stamped Strict=true; receiver gets unvalidated bytes labeled as a specific kind
findings.exploit_sketch: Sender signs Data{Type:{image,gif,strict=true}, value:path-shape}; receiver does `set %x% = %sig%` (no as-clause); BytesAsync loads PNG without throwing
findings.proposed_fix: By design — strict is process-local developer ergonomic, not cross-process contract. Trust boundary is signing (which DOES cover the Strict flag). No fix; documented as standing note.

findings.preconditions: Attacker has timing oracle on Verify and controls the comparison target
findings.impact: Theoretical timing leak; not practically exploitable for SHA-256/keccak256 due to pre-image resistance
findings.exploit_sketch: Repeatedly call verify with adaptive hash inputs and measure response time — but no useful information leaks for cryptographic-strength digests
findings.proposed_fix: Switch to CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals if/when PLang grows MAC verification (same key, attacker-controlled input). Not needed today.

summary: PASS. type-kind-strict + lazy-deserialize merged at ced2a2517. Strict×lazy seam independently re-traced: the IKindValidatable probe block at set.cs:181-195 forces Value.Value materialisation (RawUntouched=false) BEFORE the lazy-passthrough check, closing the bypass that would otherwise let a wire-strict image store raw with no imprint. Reflection-ctor probe is narrow today (image only); forward-looking discipline (IKindValidatable ctors must be side-effect free) recorded for memory. One new low-severity semgrep hit (text.Convert default-options) with a two-line fix. Two info/low standing notes (process-local imprint, Span.SequenceEqual on hash compare). No critical/high open.
